title: Cumulative Review: Triage a Failing Public Site
summary: Cumulative Review: Triage a Failing Public Site uses an original HTML incident review scenario to retrieve earlier HTML skills, add a bounded artifact layer, and verify transfer with source and browser evidence.
objectives: Retrieve html document boilerplate, html elements nesting, html attributes, html text headings lists, html links urls, html images alt, html svg, html audio, html video, html iframe, html validation tools without step-by-step imitation.
Produce and verify a real HTML incident review artifact or evidence report.
Preserve earlier valid requirements while diagnosing changed content and constraints.

steps.title: Predict 1: heat-relief information hub launch desk · review
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 1: Which claim survives a changed heat-relief information hub launch desk case with incomplete visual evidence? Work context: HTML incident review.
steps.why: Cumulative Review: Triage a Failing Public Site requires this decision because each decision must preserve document meaning, user control, portability, and inspectable evidence. This is checkpoint 1 in the HTML incident review.

content.title: HTML incident review · evidence decision
content.text: Commit before feedback, then connect the selected claim to the HTML incident review evidence.

hints: Cumulative Review: Triage a Failing Public Site: first isolate the evidence required by “Predict 1: heat-relief information hub launch desk.”
Cumulative Review: Triage a Failing Public Site: compare the current artifact with the named predict goal for step 1.
Cumulative Review: Triage a Failing Public Site: use the smallest defensible change or claim that satisfies “Predict 1: heat-relief information hub launch desk.”

options.text: HTML incident review case 1: a visible heading supplies the browser-tab title, language, encoding, and mobile viewport automatically.
HTML incident review case 1: the document needs an explicit doctype, root language, head metadata, title, viewport instruction, and body content.
HTML incident review case 1: a body element alone is a complete production document because browsers repair every missing signal identically.

options.text: HTML incident review phase 2.1: choose an element that represents the outer relationship
HTML incident review phase 2.2: open the parent before its children
HTML incident review phase 2.3: complete and close each child in order
HTML incident review phase 2.4: close the parent after its final child

options.text: HTML incident review case 3: every attribute becomes more effective when given the text value true.
HTML incident review case 3: attribute syntax and values must follow the contract defined for that element and attribute.
HTML incident review case 3: any invented attribute changes native browser behavior if its name sounds descriptive.

options.text: HTML incident review case 5: click here is always sufficient link text because users read every surrounding sentence first.
HTML incident review case 5: the href must use the right URL form and the link text should identify the destination or action.
HTML incident review case 5: every internal project link needs the author’s full local file-system path.

options.text: HTML incident review case 6: every image needs a long visual description, including decorative flourishes already ignored by sighted readers.
HTML incident review case 6: classify the image purpose first, then provide the shortest equivalent that preserves that purpose.
HTML incident review case 6: the filename is always an adequate alternative because it identifies the asset.

options.text: HTML incident review phase 7.1: decide whether the vector is content, a control, or decoration
HTML incident review phase 7.2: choose external image or inline SVG behavior
HTML incident review phase 7.3: set a viewBox that contains the drawing coordinates
HTML incident review phase 7.4: name meaningful vectors or hide decorative ones

options.text: HTML incident review case 8: once an audio file plays, controls, fallback, and a transcript are optional accessibility extras.
HTML incident review case 8: usable audio needs controls, supported sources, fallback, and an equivalent transcript.
HTML incident review case 8: a one-sentence summary is always equivalent to a detailed emergency advisory.

options.text: HTML incident review case 10: the iframe title edits the heading shown inside the remote page, so it can be omitted when that page has an h1.
HTML incident review case 10: the parent document must name the iframe, constrain its permissions, and make its box responsive.
HTML incident review case 10: removing all constraints is safest because embedded content needs every browser capability.

Cumulative Review: Triage a Failing Public Site: use the smallest defensible change or claim that satisfies “Predict 11: pre-publication conformance review.”

options.text: HTML incident review case 11: if the page looks correct in one browser, the HTML is valid and interoperable.
HTML incident review case 11: rendering and conformance are different evidence; validate source and trace each message to a root cause.
HTML incident review case 11: repair every visible symptom with extra div elements without reading validator output.

options.text: HTML incident review phase 12.1: declare the HTML parsing mode
HTML incident review phase 12.2: open the root element and identify its language
HTML incident review phase 12.3: provide machine-readable metadata and a title in head
HTML incident review phase 12.4: place the public information inside body

options.text: HTML incident review case 13: indenting a paragraph beneath main makes it a child even if the tags close out of order.
HTML incident review case 13: parent-child relationships come from correctly nested tags; indentation should then mirror that structure.
HTML incident review case 13: all elements are void elements, so closing tags are optional whenever the page still appears.
